THOUGHT:
Paper 0 is the seminal ICLR 2019 work by Hendrycks & Dietterich that introduced ImageNet-C and ImageNet-P, establishing the standard benchmark for evaluating deep learning models against common, real-world corruptions (e.g., noise, blur, weather, compression). This directly addresses the missing citation category for established robustness evaluation methodologies and natural perturbation benchmarks. It complements the existing WILDS and shortcut learning references by focusing on environmental/pixel-level shifts rather than semantic/domain shifts. I will select this paper and update the description to reflect its specific contributions and intended placement in the manuscript.
